pattern: Storing per-index only the snapshot ids at which a value changed, then binary searching that history, makes snapshots cheap instead of copying the whole array each time.
hints: Copying the entire array on every snap is wasteful when only a few cells change; record changes per index instead.
For each index keep a list of (snap_id, value) records, appended only when a set actually happens at the current snapshot.
A get becomes a predecessor search over that index's records for the largest snap_id at or below the requested one, defaulting to 0 when there is none.

edge_inputs.description: snapshot freezes a value before it is overwritten
untouched index reads as zero at an old snapshot
multiple sets between snaps keep only the latest per snapshot
later snapshots see newer writes, earlier ones do not
several indices tracked independently across snapshots

stress.description: random sets, snaps, and gets bounded to valid indices and snapshot ids
stress.note: get is only emitted once at least one snapshot exists so snap_id stays valid
